title: Stack (Java / Python)
general_notes: No double deduction (except in point 5). Do not deduct again for an error already penalised in an earlier point. For example: if a student uses the wrong operand order in point 4, base point 5 marks on whether they apply that order consistently across all operators. HOWEVER, for the overall correctness (point 5), there is double deduction for an incorrect result even if earlier steps were internally consistent.
This rubric applies to both Java and Python submissions. Any correct implementation that processes the input left to right and uses the provided stack is acceptable.
The use of 'import' is not allowed. Penalise in point 5 if 'import' appears in the submission.
Integer division (rounded down / floor) is required for the '/' operator.

rubric_points.name: Iteration and token classification
rubric_points.target: The method iterates through all tokens in the input from left to right, and distinguishes numeric tokens from operator tokens.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No iteration over the input, or all tokens are treated identically with no distinction between numbers and operators.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Iteration is present but incomplete (e.g., does not process all tokens, or skips the first/last token), or classification logic is partially correct (e.g., only operators are recognised, numbers are not handled separately).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: All tokens are processed left to right, and the method correctly distinguishes numeric tokens from operator tokens as a basis for the two different actions.

rubric_points.name: Number handling: push to stack
rubric_points.target: When a numeric token is encountered, it is converted to an integer and pushed onto the stack.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Numeric tokens are not pushed onto the stack, or are pushed without conversion (e.g., pushed as a string).

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Numeric tokens are pushed but conversion is incorrect or missing in some cases (e.g., only works for single-digit numbers, or conversion is applied to operators as well).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Numeric tokens are correctly converted to integers and pushed onto the stack.

rubric_points.name: Operator handling: pop, compute, push
rubric_points.target: When an operator token is encountered, exactly two values are popped from the stack, the operation is applied, and the result is pushed back. This is evaluated independently of operand order.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: No stack operations are performed for operators, or only one value is popped, or the result is not pushed back onto the stack.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Two values are popped and a result is pushed, but the computation step is missing or incorrect for some operators (e.g., always adds regardless of operator, or handles only some operators). Operand order is evaluated separately in point 4.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: For every operator token, exactly two values are popped, the correct arithmetic operation is applied for all four operators, and the result is pushed back. Operand order is evaluated separately in point 4.

rubric_points.name: Correct operand order for non-commutative operators (- and /)
rubric_points.target: For subtraction and division, the first value popped from the stack is the right operand and the second popped is the left operand (i.e., result = second_popped op first_popped). This matches RPN semantics.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Operand order is reversed for subtraction and/or division (e.g., computes first_popped - second_popped), producing incorrect results for non-symmetric expressions.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Operand order is correct for one of subtraction or division but wrong for the other, or the order is correct but integer division is not applied (e.g., uses floating-point division).

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Both subtraction and division use the correct operand order: second_popped op first_popped. Integer (floor) division is applied for '/'.

rubric_points.name: Overall correctness
rubric_points.target: The method returns the correct integer result for all valid RPN expressions, including expressions with all four operators and multiple nested operations.

buckets.label: Wrong
buckets.description: Method fails to compile or run, or produces incorrect output for most test cases.

buckets.label: Semi
buckets.description: Method produces correct results for simple expressions (e.g., single operator) but fails for more complex inputs, or fails specifically for subtraction and division due to operand order.

buckets.label: Correct
buckets.description: Method produces the correct result for all test cases, including complex nested expressions and all four operators.
